name: Danae
description: Danae, daughter of King Acrisius of Argos, is a pivotal figure in Greek mythology, primarily known as the mother of Perseus by Zeus. King Acrisius, fearing a prophecy that he would be killed by his own grandson, imprisoned Danae in a bronze chamber or tower to prevent her from conceiving. However, Zeus, captivated by her beauty, visited her in the form of golden rain, impregnating her. When Perseus was born, Acrisius, still fearing the prophecy, cast Danae and her infant son adrift in a wooden chest at sea. They were rescued by Dictys, a fisherman on the island of Seriphos. Danae raised Perseus there, protecting him from the unwanted advances of King Polydectes. Perseus eventually fulfilled the prophecy by accidentally killing Acrisius with a discus during athletic games. Danae's story highlights themes of fate, divine intervention, and the power of motherhood. Her resilience in the face of adversity and her role as the mother of a great hero solidified her place in Greek mythology. Her tale demonstrates the inescapable nature of prophecy and the limitations of human attempts to defy the will of the gods. Danae's legacy endures through Perseus's heroic deeds and her own symbolic representation of beauty and divine favor. She represents both vulnerability and strength, a mortal woman chosen by a god and enduring hardship to protect her child. Her story serves as a powerful reminder of the complexities of human and divine relationships in Greek mythology.
subtitle: Mother of Perseus, favored by Zeus

keyMyths.title: Imprisonment and Golden Rain
keyMyths.description: Danae was imprisoned by her father, Acrisius, who feared a prophecy. Zeus impregnated her in the form of golden rain, circumventing her captivity.

keyMyths.title: Exposure at Sea
keyMyths.description: Acrisius cast Danae and Perseus adrift in a chest at sea, fearing the prophecy. They were rescued by Dictys on the island of Seriphos.

keyMyths.title: Life on Seriphos
keyMyths.description: Danae raised Perseus on Seriphos, protecting him from King Polydectes. She played a crucial role in Perseus's development into a hero.

sources.author: Apollodorus
sources.description: A comprehensive account of Greek mythology.
sources.text: A comprehensive account of Greek mythology.

sources.author: Ovid
sources.description: A poetic retelling of Greek myths.
